case_summary: A complaint has been filed with the Haryana Police regarding theft of various items, including conveyance, tools, and metal goods, valued at Rs. 30,000. The incident occurred on 15/03/2026 in the area of Dhansauli, near Gharhi Bessak Road, under the jurisdiction of Sanoli Police Station, Panipat district.

sections.title: Punishment for theft
sections.description: Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.

sections.title: Theft of property from a specified category
sections.description: Whoever commits theft of property from a specified category shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.

sop.action: Register FIR immediately under BNSS Section 173
sop.file_url: sop/Regulation No. 14 of 2024.GUIDLINE FOR EXPEDITIOUS & FAIR INVESTIGATION OF RAPE CASES.pdf

sc_judgments.case_name: Landmark Supreme Court Directives & NHRC Guidelines
sc_judgments.guideline: FIR must be registered immediately on receipt of cognizable offense
sc_judgments.holding: The Constitution Bench unanimously held that registration of FIR is mandatory under Section 173 BNSS if the information discloses commission of a cognizable offence.
key_points: FIR cannot be refused on any pretext for cognizable offences
Preliminary inquiry can only be done in cases like matrimonial disputes, commercial cases, medical negligence, corruption - not in serious crimes
Officer refusing to register FIR is liable under departmental action and contempt of court
Victim can approach SP or Magistrate if SHO refuses
sc_judgments.io_duty: Mandatory - Register FIR immediately. No preliminary inquiry for cognizable offences. Non-compliance invites disciplinary action.

evidence_checklist: Photograph all visible evidence of theft
Record witness statements under BNSS Sec 180
io_warnings: MANDATORY: Do not refuse FIR - Landmark SC judgment applies
